# gamereel

- purpose: Generate short-form videos (TikTok / IG Reels) from game protocol replays
- language: Rust
- workspace_layout: crates/{gamereel-core, proto-puzzle, proto-bubble} + apps/{gamereel-cli, hs-mvp}
- plugin_mechanism: ProtocolParser trait + inventory::submit! self-registration; one crate per game protocol

## Workspace members

### crates/gamereel-core (v0.1.0)
- purpose: Video generation engine + ProtocolParser trait
- modules: encoder_profile, encoder_pick, ffmpeg_inc/{mod,hwctx,stage_mgr,texture,image_effect,stream}, cuda_pipeline/{mod,kernels}, stage/{scene,node,action,model}, protocol
- pipelines:
  - sws_scale (CPU): create_scene_stream / create_scene_stream_with_profile
  - CUDA hwframes (GPU): create_scene_stream_cuda
- entry_types: GamereelError (was MovieError), GamereelResult (was MoveMakerResult)
- profiles: EncoderProfile::{Fast, Balanced, TikTokHQ, IgReelsHDR}
- deps: ffmpeg-next (git), ffmpeg-sys-next 8.1, cudarc 0.19 (dynamic-loading), image, imageproc, tween, tokio, serde, inventory, env_logger, thiserror

### crates/proto-puzzle (v0.1.0)
- purpose: Protocol parser for puzzle-style games (block matching, falling pieces)
- status: skeleton — registers a placeholder PuzzleParser that produces a constant ParsedReplay
- registers: ProtocolDescriptor { name: "puzzle", … } via inventory::submit!

### crates/proto-bubble (v0.1.0)
- purpose: Protocol parser for bubble-shooter games
- status: skeleton — same shape as proto-puzzle
- registers: ProtocolDescriptor { name: "bubble", … } via inventory::submit!

### apps/gamereel-cli (v0.1.0)
- binary: gamereel
- subcommands: list-protocols, render --protocol <name> --input <file>
- json_flag: --json (matches CLAUDE.md repo convention)
- force_links: `use proto_puzzle as _;` and `use proto_bubble as _;` to keep inventory constructors after lto=fat

### apps/hs-mvp (v0.1.0)
- purpose: Hearthstone-style match recap demo (original demo, kept as integration example)
- depends_on: gamereel-core (path)

## Performance milestones

- M0 baseline (libx264 medium): 152 fps e2e, VMAF 99.34
- M1 (encoder auto-pick + scaler hoist): 377 fps (2.48× M0)
- M2 (4 quality profiles + 144-pt VMAF grid): 381 fps, all profiles VMAF ≥ 92
- M3 (cudarc RGBA→NV12 + ffmpeg CUDA hwframes): 456 fps (1.23× M2), 0 MB VRAM leak / 100 cycles
- M4 target: wgpu compositor (replace CPU image_effect.rs) — ≥ 1500 fps e2e
- M5 target: actix actor pool with hardware-bounded concurrency

## Build

```bash
cargo build --workspace --release
cargo test  --workspace
cargo run   -p gamereel-cli -- list-protocols
cargo run   -p gamereel-cli -- render --protocol puzzle --input some.bin
```

## System requirements

- FFmpeg dev libraries (libavcodec-dev libavformat-dev libavfilter-dev libavutil-dev libswscale-dev)
- clang, pkg-config
- For CUDA pipeline (M3+): NVIDIA driver ≥ 535, libnvrtc12, libnvrtc-builtins12.0
- For quality benches: vmaf (Netflix libvmaf 3.x), jq

## Documentation

- README.md / README_CN.md — human-readable overview
- docs/optimization-log.md — every perf change with hypothesis + self-proof test + measurement + retro
- benches/results/m{0,1,2,3}.json — milestone trend artifacts
